steps.prompt: Which word is the first main verb?

hints.togae: 'togae' is a noun meaning 'to the toga'

hints.to cut: 'to cut' is 'caedere' — 'cedere' (to yield) has a different vowel
hints.to fall: 'to fall' is 'cadere' — similar sounding but different from 'cedere'

hints.Present indicative: Present indicative would be 'cedunt' — 'cedant' with -a- is subjunctive (jussive)
hints.Future indicative: Future would be 'cedent' — 'cedant' is a present subjunctive expressing a command
hints.Imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'cederent' — 'cedant' is present subjunctive

hints.caedo, caedere, cecidi, caesum: These are the parts of 'caedere' (to cut/kill) — not 'cedere' (to yield)
hints.cado, cadere, cecidi, casum: These are the parts of 'cadere' (to fall) — different from 'cedere'
hints.cano, canere, cecini, cantum: These are the parts of 'canere' (to sing) — unrelated to 'cedere'

hints.to conceal: 'to conceal' is 'celare' — 'concedere' means to yield or grant

hints.Present indicative: Present indicative would be 'concedit' — 'concedat' with -a- is subjunctive
hints.Future indicative: Future would be 'concedet' — 'concedat' is present subjunctive (jussive)
hints.Perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'concesserit' — 'concedat' is present

hints.concido, concidere, concidi, concisum: These are the parts of 'concidere' (to cut down) — not 'concedere'
hints.condo, condere, condidi, conditum: These are the parts of 'condere' (to found/store) — different verb
hints.confero, conferre, contuli, collatum: These are the parts of 'conferre' (to bring together) — not 'concedere'

hints.Accusative: 'arma' is neuter plural — nominative and accusative are identical, but as subject of 'cedant' it is nominative
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural would be 'armis' — 'arma' is nominative/accusative neuter plural
hints.Vocative: Cicero is not addressing weapons — 'arma' is the subject of 'cedant'

reveal.gloss: to the toga (i.e., peace / civic life)

hints.Nominative: Nominative singular is 'toga' — 'togae' here is dative (yield TO the toga)
hints.Genitive: While 'togae' can be genitive, 'cedere' takes a dative complement
hints.Vocative: Cicero is not addressing the toga — 'togae' is dative with 'cedant'

hints.2nd: 2nd declension dative would end in -o — 'toga, togae' is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension dative would end in -i — 'togae' is 1st declension
hints.5th: 5th declension dative would end in -ei — 'togae' is 1st declension

hints.Ablative: Ablative would look the same for 1st declension, but 'laurea' is the subject of 'concedat'
hints.Vocative: Cicero is not addressing the laurel — 'laurea' is the subject
hints.Accusative: Accusative singular would be 'lauream' — 'laurea' is nominative

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'laus' — 'laudi' is the dative singular
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'laudis' — 'laudi' is the dative form
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'laude' — 'laudi' is dative, parallel to 'togae'

hints.1st: 1st declension dative ends in -ae — 'laus, laudis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension dative ends in -o — 'laudi' is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension dative ends in -ui — 'laudi' is 3rd declension

translation: Let arms yield to the toga, let the laurel concede to praise.
